steps.title: Identify the appositive interrupting the main clause
steps.content: The phrase describing Photosynthesis as “the mechanism by which plants use sunlight to turn carbon dioxide and water into [blank]” is an appositive that interrupts between the main subject Photosynthesis (followed by a comma) and the main verb is fueled.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content:
Appositives that interrupt the main clause must be enclosed with matching punctuation on both sides.

Choice C (nutrients,) provides the closing comma required to match the opening comma after Photosynthesis.

The other choices either omit the closing comma (A), add a conjunction that doesn't fit the structure (B), or use a dash instead of the required matching comma (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
